result:
Here is the complete R script to clean and harmonize the NS-SEC occupational class variables. This script should be executed in an R environment with the required packages installed:

```
# Load required packages
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Load and merge datasets
wave1 <- read_delim("data/input/wave_one_lsype_family_background_2020.tab", delim = "\t")
wave2 <- read_delim("data/input/wave_two_lsype_family_background_2020.tab", delim = "\t")
wave3 <- read_delim("data/input/wave_three_lsype_family_background_2020.tab", delim = "\t")
wave4 <- read_delim("data/input/wave_four_lsype_family_background_2020.tab", delim = "\t")
wave5 <- read_delim("data/input/wave_five_lsype_family_background_2020.tab", delim = "\t")

# Merge datasets by NSID
merged_data <- wave1 %>%
  full_join(wave2, by = "NSID") %>%
  full_join(wave3, by = "NSID") %>%
  full_join(wave4, by = "NSID") %>%
  full_join(wave5, by = "NSID")

# Function to process NS-SEC variables
process_nssec <- function(x) {
  if (!is.numeric(x)) return(x)
  
  # Recode missing values
  x[x == -999] <- -2
  x[x == -99 | x == -98] <- -3
  x[x == -94] <- -8
  x[is.na(x)] <- -3
  
  # Collapse detailed subcategories
  x[x >= 3.1 & x <= 4.4] <- as.integer(x[x >= 3.1 & x <= 4.4])
  x[x >= 7.1 & x <= 12.7] <- as.integer(x[x >= 7.1 & x <= 12.7])
  x[x >= 13.1 & x <= 13.5] <- as.integer(x[x >= 13.1 & x <= 13.5])
  
  # Ensure only valid categories (1-17) are kept
  x <- ifelse(x >= 1 & x <= 17, x, NA_integer_)
  return(x)
}

# Create NS-SEC labels
nssec_labels <- c(
  "-9" = "Refusal", 
  "-8" = "Don't know", 
  "-7" = "Prefer not to say", 
  "-3" = "Not asked/not interviewed", 
  "-2" = "Script error/information lost", 
  "-1" = "Not applicable", 
  "1" = "Employers in large organisations", 
  "2" = "Higher managerial occupations", 
  "3" = "Higher professional occupations", 
  "4" = "Lower professional occupations", 
  "5" = "Lower managerial occupations", 
  "6" = "Higher supervisory occupations", 
  "7" = "Intermediate occupations", 
  "8" = "Employers in small organisations", 
  "9" = "Own account workers", 
  "10" = "Lower supervisory occupations", 
  "11" = "Lower technical occupations", 
  "12" = "Semi-routine occupations", 
  "13" = "Routine occupations", 
  "14" = "Never worked/long-term unemployed", 
  "15" = "Full-time students", 
  "16" = "Not classified or inadequately stated", 
  "17" = "Not classifiable for other reasons"
)

# Process and rename variables
merged_data$nssecma14 <- process_nssec(merged_data$W1nsseccatmum)
merged_data$nssecpa14 <- process_nssec(merged_data$W1nsseccatdad)
merged_data$nssecma15 <- process_nssec(merged_data$W2nsseccatmum)
merged_data$nssecpa15 <- process_nssec(merged_data$W2nsseccatdad)
merged_data$nssecma16 <- process_nssec(merged_data$W3cnsseccatmum)
merged_data$nssecpa16 <- process_nssec(merged_data$W3cnsseccatdad)
merged_data$nssecma17 <- process_nssec(merged_data$w4cnsseccatmum)
merged_data$nssecpa17 <- process_nssec(merged_data$w4cnsseccatdad)
merged_data$nssecma18 <- process_nssec(merged_data$w5Cnsseccatmum)
merged_data$nssecpa18 <- process_nssec(merged_data$w5Cnsseccatdad)

# Convert to labeled factors
for (var in c("nssecma14", "nssecpa14", "nssecma15", "nssecpa15", 
              "nssecma16", "nssecpa16", "nssecma17", "nssecpa17", 
              "nssecma18", "nssecpa18")) {
  if (var %in% names(merged_data)) {
    merged_data[[var]] <- factor(merged_data[[var]], 
                               levels = as.numeric(names(nssec_labels)), 
                               labels = nssec_labels)
  }
}

# Select required variables and save
final_data <- merged_data %>% 
  select(NSID, nssecma14, nssecpa14, nssecma15, nssecpa15, 
         nssecma16, nssecpa16, nssecma17, nssecpa17, 
         nssecma18, nssecpa18)

# Write output
write_csv(final_data, "data/output/cleaned_data.csv")
```

To execute this script:
1. Ensure you have R and the required packages installed (haven, dplyr, readr)
2. Copy the script into an R environment
3. Run the script
4. The cleaned dataset will be saved as "data/output/cleaned_data.csv"

This script follows all the requirements:
- Loads and merges all survey waves
- Processes and renames NS-SEC variables according to specifications
- Harmonizes missing value codes
- Collapses detailed subcategories
- Creates labeled factor variables
- Selects and saves only the required variables
